canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, multimodal understanding is predominantly framed as a latent ability or capacity to integrate and interpret information from multiple modalities, with the most common pairing being vision and language. While this vision-text focus is prevalent, a smaller body of work extends the concept to include audio, video, speech, and specialized data types such as 3D molecular structures. This construct is most commonly operationalized and measured through model performance on a wide array of benchmarks, with MMBench, MME, MMMU, and VQA-v2 appearing frequently as evaluation instruments. The tasks used to assess this ability are consistently centered on visual question answering and image captioning. Some papers conceptualize the underlying mechanism as "multimodal alignment" or "multimodal fusion," focusing on how a model creates a shared semantic space from different inputs. A few studies also posit that this construct influences downstream capabilities like multimodal generation.

definitions.text: The latent ability to understand and reason over combined text and image inputs, integrating information across modalities to perform tasks like visual question answering and captioning.
definitions.paper: DreamLLM: Synergistic Multimodal Comprehension and Creation

definitions.text: The capacity to integrate and interpret information from multiple modalities such as images, text, IDs, and tabular attributes when making predictions or generating outputs.
definitions.paper: Towards Unified Multi-Modal Personalization: Large Vision-Language Models for Generative Recommendation and Beyond

definitions.text: The latent ability to integrate and interpret information across vision and language modalities to perform tasks such as question answering and captioning.
definitions.paper: Emu: Generative Pretraining in Multimodality

definitions.text: The latent ability of a language model to understand and reason about 3D molecular structures through textual input and output, integrating spatial conformation with semantic meaning.
definitions.paper: Towards 3D Molecule-Text Interpretation in Language Models

definitions.text: The ability to align and reason across molecular structural data and natural language descriptions, enabling coherent interpretation of molecules in both modalities.
definitions.paper: Towards 3D Molecule-Text Interpretation in Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to interpret molecular structures from 3D geometry and use that information to answer questions or generate descriptions.
definitions.paper: Towards 3D Molecule-Text Interpretation in Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to integrate and interpret information from both visual and linguistic modalities, enabling coherent responses to image-based queries.
definitions.paper: MiniGPT-4: Enhancing Vision-Language Understanding with Advanced Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to understand and align information from both visual and textual modalities to perform complex tasks.
definitions.paper: Vision-Language Foundation Models as Effective Robot Imitators

definitions.text: The underlying capacity of a multimodal model to integrate and interpret information from both visual and linguistic inputs to form coherent, context-sensitive responses or actions.
definitions.paper: Guiding Instruction-based Image Editing via Multimodal Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to integrate auditory and visual information to comprehend videos accurately across multimodal and unimodal settings.
definitions.paper: Aligned Better, Listen Better for Audio-Visual Large Language Models

definitions.text: A broad set of skills for processing and reasoning about information from multiple modalities, such as text and images, in non-specialized contexts.
definitions.paper: Harnessing Webpage UIs for Text-Rich Visual Understanding

definitions.text: The latent capacity to process and respond appropriately to image-based or other non-text inputs after vision-language adaptation.
definitions.paper: How Does Vision-Language Adaptation Impact the Safety of Vision Language Models?

definitions.text: The overall proficiency of a model across a wide range of tasks that require processing and integrating both visual and textual information.
definitions.paper: PerturboLLaVA: Reducing Multimodal Hallucinations with Perturbative Visual Training

definitions.text: The general ability of the model to process and integrate information from multiple modalities, such as vision and language.
definitions.paper: See What You Are Told: Visual Attention Sink in Large Multimodal Models

definitions.text: The ability of a model to understand and reason over long-form content where text and multiple images are intertwined, requiring it to identify relevant information while ignoring distractors.
definitions.paper: Learning Interleaved Image-Text Comprehension in Vision-Language Large Models

definitions.text: The model's ability to answer questions and solve problems across a wide range of academic and professional subjects.
definitions.paper: Feast Your Eyes:  Mixture-of-Resolution Adaptation for Multimodal Large Language Models

definitions.text: The model's latent ability to comprehend and integrate information from both visual (images) and textual modalities.
definitions.paper: REMEDY: Recipe Merging Dynamics in Large Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: The ability to correctly interpret and extract meaning from queries that contain a mix of text and images.
definitions.paper: MMIE: Massive Multimodal Interleaved Comprehension Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to understand information presented in alternating image and text sequences and integrate it into a coherent interpretation.
definitions.paper: MMIE: Massive Multimodal Interleaved Comprehension Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to jointly understand visual scenes and natural-language prompts in order to ground language in image content.
definitions.paper: SegLLM: Multi-round Reasoning Segmentation with Large Language Models

definitions.text: The tendency to follow natural-language instructions in a multimodal setting, including responding in the requested format.
definitions.paper: Dysca: A Dynamic and Scalable Benchmark for Evaluating Perception Ability of LVLMs

definitions.text: The latent ability of a vision-language model to consistently and appropriately integrate visual and linguistic information in a manner aligned with human expectations across diverse inputs and dialogue turns.
definitions.paper: Unsolvable Problem Detection: Robust Understanding Evaluation for Large Multimodal Models

definitions.text: The process of mapping and connecting representations from different modalities, such as vision and language, to create a shared semantic space.
definitions.paper: EfficientQAT: Efficient Quantization-Aware Training for Large Language Models

definitions.text: The model's internal capability to effectively integrate and process information from different modalities, such as text and images, to form a unified understanding.
definitions.paper: Linguistic Generalizability of Test-Time Scaling in Mathematical Reasoning

definitions.text: The latent ability to synthesize and reason over both textual and visual inputs in a coordinated way to produce accurate medical interpretations.
definitions.paper: DEEPERInsight into Your User: Directed Persona Refinement for Dynamic Persona Modeling

definitions.text: The degree to which a model integrates and coherently aligns information from different modalities (e.g., vision and language), ensuring that outputs are grounded in and consistent with all input modalities.
definitions.paper: A Training-freeLLM-based Approach to GeneralChinese Character Error Correction

definitions.text: The ability to comprehend and reason over inputs that simultaneously combine text, image, and speech modalities.
definitions.paper: InMind: EvaluatingLLMs in Capturing and Applying Individual Human Reasoning Styles

definitions.text: The model's general ability to comprehend the semantic relationship and alignment between visual and textual information.
definitions.paper: TripletCLIP:  Improving Compositional Reasoning of CLIP via Synthetic Vision-Language Negatives

definitions.text: Producing correct answers or outputs on image-based benchmarks that require interpreting visual inputs.
definitions.paper: Should VLMs be Pre-trained with Image Data?

characterization: Across the surveyed literature, multimodal understanding is predominantly framed as a latent ability or capacity to integrate and interpret information from multiple modalities, with the most common pairing being vision and language. While this vision-text focus is prevalent, a smaller body of work extends the concept to include audio, video, speech, and specialized data types such as 3D molecular structures. This construct is most commonly operationalized and measured through model performance on a wide array of benchmarks, with MMBench, MME, MMMU, and VQA-v2 appearing frequently as evaluation instruments. The tasks used to assess this ability are consistently centered on visual question answering and image captioning. Some papers conceptualize the underlying mechanism as "multimodal alignment" or "multimodal fusion," focusing on how a model creates a shared semantic space from different inputs. A few studies also posit that this construct influences downstream capabilities like multimodal generation.
